name: tilted_approach

symptoms: object gets pushed away during a vertical top-down descent instead of being straddled
gripper closes on air just above the object although the tip midpoint XY matches the object
trace_evidence: contact pair palm/Fixed_Jaw <-> object appears during descent (impulse ~0.2-0.5) while tips are still above the grasp height
descent stalls: tip z stops ~30-70 mm above the IK target

when_to_apply: The gripper palm has geometry (central spike/frame) that protrudes toward the tool axis between the fingers — a true vertical approach bottoms the palm on the object top before the fingers straddle it.
repair_strategy: Tilt the approach axis back from vertical so the palm clears the object top while the jaws still straddle it. For the roboninecom parallel gripper on AlohaMini, pitch 55-70 deg (from horizontal) all succeed; 45 deg gets marginal, 90 deg (vertical) fails. Back off along the tilted axis for the pre-grasp, and descend ALONG that axis.
code_sketch: d = desired_approach_dir(obj_pos, pitch_deg=60)     # down-and-forward unit vector
validated_on: AlohaMini Pro: rubiks cube at 55/60/65/70 (9/9 showcase clips success)
